repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
continues https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/39166

## Problem

- Too much boilerplate needed to implement vimfns and excmds in Lua.
- `f_environ` could be implemented fully in Lua.

## Solution

- Set `func_lua` and move its impl to `_core/vimfn.lua`
    - Caveat: since `create_environment` called `f_environ` from C, it had to be migrated to use `nlua_call_vimfn` to invoke `vimfn.lua:f_environ`.
- Use `nlua_call_vimfn` from `f_serverlist` to reduce boilerplate.
- Use `nlua_call_excmd` to impl `ex_log`, `ex_lsp`.

comment_data.body: does the lua version need this? :< presumably not, since that would mean that `uv.os_environ()` is buggy on Windows.

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1629,6 +1629,52 @@ Object nlua_exec(const String str, const char *chunkname, const Array args, LuaR
   return nlua_call_pop_retval(lstate, mode, arena, top, err);
 }
 
+/// Call require(module).func(argvars...), store result in rettv.
+///
+/// Converts argvars directly to Lua values (no Object intermediate), calls the
+/// Lua function, and converts the result directly back to typval_T.
+///
+/// @param module   Lua module name, e.g. "vim._core.server".
+/// @param func     Function name in the module, e.g. "serverlist".
+/// @param argvars  typval_T arguments (VAR_UNKNOWN-terminated).
+/// @param[out] rettv  Return value. Set to VAR_NIL on error.
+/// @return true on success, false on error (message already emitted).
+bool nlua_call_module_typval(const char *module, const char *func,

comment_data.body:
Now `f_serverlist` is implemented entirely in Lua, except for the call to `server_address_list`, and there is almost no boilerplate.

`nlua_call_module_typval` (whatever it ends up being called) hopefully will allow almost any `f_xx` function to put most of their logic in Lua (except for cases where C is obviously more appropriate).
From memory I thought `uv.os_environ` wasn't exactly equivalent and the vim version has some special handling for HOME or something? I can't fully remember though.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -11,4 +11,10 @@ function M.f_hostname()
   return vim.uv.os_gethostname()
 end
 
+--- Returns all environment variables as a dictionary.
+--- @return table<string, string>
+function M.f_environ()
+  return vim.uv.os_environ()

comment_data.body:
That is the case for `vim_getenv`, which is used by `fn.getenv()`, but there was no such handling in `f_environ`.

As a followup, making that "work" for `vim.env` etc. might be a good idea, and would then allow `fn.getenv` / `fn.setenv` to be replaced. That's more of a backlog item though.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -11,4 +11,10 @@ function M.f_hostname()
   return vim.uv.os_gethostname()
 end
 
+--- Returns all environment variables as a dictionary.
+--- @return table<string, string>
+function M.f_environ()
+  return vim.uv.os_environ()

comment_data.body: Nice! You love to see it.

labels.description: changes that are not features or bugfixes
labels.name: refactor

title: refactor(vimfn): full-Lua impl of vim.fn.environ()
